title: Automated Bot
description: A wall-following autonomous bot that maintains distance, detects parcel boxes, counts laps via IR strip sensing, and displays stats on an LCD.
detailedDescription:
This project is an autonomous wall-following robot designed for navigation and tracking tasks in controlled corridors. It uses closed-loop feedback to hold a stable offset from walls while scanning for marked objects.

How it works:
- Dual Sharp sensors continuously measure wall distance
- The controller computes correction values in real time
- IR strip detection marks lap completion points
- Object checks are logged while moving through each segment

System capabilities:
- Distance control using dual Sharp sensors
- Lap tracking through IR strip detection
- Real-time counters shown on a 16x2 LCD
- Arduino-based control pipeline for reliable behavior

Impact:
This bot serves as a practical base for warehouse-path prototypes and autonomous corridor navigation tasks where repeatability and clear monitoring are essential.

title: Mark 1.6
description: A fully wireless Bluetooth-controlled robotic arm mounted on a 4WD chassis, built with 3D-printed components and NRF communication modules.
detailedDescription:
Mark 1.6 combines mobility and manipulation in one platform. A 3D-printed robotic arm is integrated with a 4WD base, enabling both movement and pick-and-place style operations through wireless control.

Design approach:
- Compact arm geometry optimized for lightweight movement
- Wireless stack to separate control logic from hardware wiring constraints
- Chassis-arm integration tuned for center-of-mass stability

System capabilities:
- Bluetooth and NRF-based wireless command flow
- 3D-printed arm structure for rapid prototyping
- 4WD chassis for stable movement
- Practical platform for control and mechatronics testing

Why it matters:
This platform is ideal for demonstrating coordinated locomotion + manipulation, which is a core requirement in field robotics and assisted automation systems.

title: Line Follower Bot
description: A mobile robot that detects and follows floor tracks using IR sensors, onboard processing, and motor driver control for autonomous path following.
detailedDescription:
The Line Follower Bot is built for autonomous path tracing on high-contrast tracks. Sensor readings are continuously processed to adjust wheel speed and steering in real time.

Control workflow:
- IR array samples line position at high frequency
- Error between centerline and robot orientation is computed
- Motor PWM values are adjusted dynamically to reduce drift
- Smoother turns are achieved through tuned correction thresholds

System capabilities:
- IR sensor array for line detection
- Fast motor correction loop for stable tracking
- Embedded control for route adherence
- Competition-friendly design for speed and precision

Learning outcome:
This project provides strong fundamentals in sensor-based feedback, tuning, and real-time embedded decision-making.

title: PlutoX
description: An open-source nano drone with Wi-Fi camera support, manual app control, and ROS-based scripting for computer-vision and automation workflows.
detailedDescription:
PlutoX is a lightweight programmable drone used to explore ROS workflows, image processing, and autonomous flight logic. It supports manual control as well as script-driven behavior.

Development focus:
- Rapid integration between camera stream and perception modules
- Script-based mission logic for waypoint and task automation
- Human-in-loop fallback for safer real-world testing

System capabilities:
- 720p camera stream for CV experiments
- ROS integration for automation pipelines
- Manual app control for field testing
- Suitable for surveillance and mapping prototypes

Future scope:
The same platform can be extended to traffic analytics, search-and-assist scenarios, and autonomous monitoring pipelines.

title: 3D Printing
description: Additive manufacturing workflows used to convert digital models into physical prototypes by depositing material layer by layer.
detailedDescription:
This initiative focuses on additive manufacturing for rapid robotics prototyping. Digital CAD models are converted into physical components through layered deposition.

Workflow:
- CAD design and tolerance validation
- Slicer configuration for layer quality and infill strength
- Iterative prints with fit and load testing
- Revision cycle based on assembly performance

System capabilities:
- Faster iteration for custom robot parts
- Reduced fabrication cost for small batches
- Better fit-checking before final builds
- Supports arm joints, mounts, brackets, and enclosures

Practical value:
3D printing significantly shortens build cycles and enables low-cost experimentation for student-led hardware innovation.

title: Self Balancing Bot
description: An inverted-pendulum robot that stays upright by driving its wheels in the direction of tilt using MPU6050 sensor feedback and control logic.
detailedDescription:
The Self Balancing Bot demonstrates inverted-pendulum stabilization. Sensor fusion and control tuning are used to keep the center of mass aligned above the wheel axis.

Control pipeline:
- IMU data is sampled for tilt and angular velocity
- Error from target upright angle is calculated continuously
- Motor commands are generated through tuned feedback gains
- Disturbance recovery is improved via iterative calibration

System capabilities:
- MPU6050-based tilt sensing
- Real-time correction through motor drive
- PID-style control behavior for stability
- Strong learning platform for dynamics and control theory

Engineering value:
This project is a compact and powerful testbed for understanding stability, control loops, and real-time corrective actuation.

title: Radar Navigation
description: A marine-navigation study project focused on radar-based vessel detection, tracking, and positioning using X-band and S-band systems.
detailedDescription:
Radar Navigation is a domain study project centered on vessel identification and safe route awareness in maritime environments. It compares X-band and S-band behavior across weather and visibility conditions.

Study coverage:
- Radar-based target detection and echo interpretation
- Relative motion understanding for collision avoidance
- Environmental influence on signal reliability
- Practical differences between X-band and S-band operation

Key highlights:
- Vessel tracking and positioning concepts
- X-band for sharper target resolution
- S-band for reliability in rain and fog
- Safety-focused interpretation aligned with navigation rules

Outcome:
The project builds a clear conceptual base for safe marine navigation, situational awareness, and radar-assisted decision support.

title: Hexapod
description: A six-legged robotic platform designed for stable multi-axis motion using Arduino Nano, SG90 servos, PCA9685 driver, and 3D-printed parts.
detailedDescription:
Hexapod is a six-legged walking robot created for stable locomotion and gait experimentation. Its modular frame and servo architecture support repeatable motion studies.

Locomotion strategy:
- Coordinated leg sequencing for tripod-style movement
- Servo timing optimization for smooth transitions
- Balance maintenance during turns and uneven motion
- Mechanical tuning for reduced wobble and energy loss

Key highlights:
- 6-DOF locomotion behavior with gait patterns
- Arduino Nano + PCA9685 servo orchestration
- SG90 servo-based joint control
- 3D-printed chassis for lightweight structure

Why this project matters:
It introduces core concepts of legged robotics, gait planning, and motion control beyond conventional wheeled platforms.

title: Air Quality Monitoring Drone
description: A manually controlled drone that captures atmospheric gas data via MQ2 and uploads telemetry to Firebase using ESP8266 for remote monitoring.
detailedDescription:
This drone platform is designed for air-quality monitoring in zones that are hard to inspect manually. It captures gas concentration data and pushes live telemetry to cloud storage.

Operational flow:
- Drone captures region-specific air samples during flight
- MQ2 sensor readings are processed onboard
- ESP8266 publishes timestamped values to Firebase
- Remote dashboard enables live monitoring and analysis

Key highlights:
- MQ2 sensing for gases like LPG, methane, and CO
- ESP8266 pipeline for Firebase updates
- Field-deployable multirotor stack for data capture
- Useful for urban monitoring and hazardous area checks

Deployment value:
The system supports data-driven environmental checks for campuses, industrial zones, and difficult-to-access locations.
